findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns where remote content is piped directly to a shell interpreter. This allows arbitrary code execution from a remote server. Frequently seen in malicious skills that download and execute payloads.
findings.reportTitle: Remote code download and execution detected
findings.recommendation: This code downloads content from the internet and executes it directly. An attacker can change the remote payload at any time. This is one of the most dangerous patterns in agent skills. Do not install.

fullDescription.text: Detects patterns where remote content is piped directly to a shell interpreter. This allows arbitrary code execution from a remote server. Frequently seen in malicious skills that download and execute payloads.

message.text: Remote code download and execution detected

properties.recommendation: This code downloads content from the internet and executes it directly. An attacker can change the remote payload at any time. This is one of the most dangerous patterns in agent skills. Do not install.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests to domains that don't match the tool's declared purpose. MCP servers making unexpected network calls may be exfiltrating data or phoning home to C2 infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Review all network calls. Verify each URL belongs to the service the tool is supposed to interact with. Unexpected POST requests are the highest-risk pattern.

matches.description: HTTP requests to obviously suspicious domains

fullDescription.text: Detects outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests to domains that don't match the tool's declared purpose. MCP servers making unexpected network calls may be exfiltrating data or phoning home to C2 infrastructure.

matchDetails.description: HTTP requests to obviously suspicious domains

properties.recommendation: Review all network calls. Verify each URL belongs to the service the tool is supposed to interact with. Unexpected POST requests are the highest-risk pattern.

findings.ruleTitle: File size anomaly for file type
findings.ruleDescription: Flags files whose size significantly exceeds the expected range for their file type. A tailwind.config.js at 7KB when typical is 1-2KB, or a SKILL.md at 50KB when typical is 2-5KB, suggests hidden content.
findings.reportTitle: File size or structure anomaly detected
findings.recommendation: Inspect the file for hidden content, especially at the end of long lines or after the apparent end of meaningful code.

matches.description: Config file more than 8x expected size for its type

rules.name: File size anomaly for file type

shortDescription.text: File size anomaly for file type

fullDescription.text: Flags files whose size significantly exceeds the expected range for their file type. A tailwind.config.js at 7KB when typical is 1-2KB, or a SKILL.md at 50KB when typical is 2-5KB, suggests hidden content.

message.text: File size or structure anomaly detected

matchDetails.description: Config file more than 8x expected size for its type

properties.recommendation: Inspect the file for hidden content, especially at the end of long lines or after the apparent end of meaningful code.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns where data is intercepted, copied, or forwarded to a secondary endpoint while still providing normal responses. This dual-channel approach is used by sophisticated malware to avoid detection — the tool works correctly while silently exfiltrating data.
findings.reportTitle: Data interception / dual-channel exfiltration pattern detected
findings.recommendation: This code appears to intercept, copy, or forward data to a hidden secondary endpoint while maintaining normal operation. This is the pattern used in the Postmark-MCP typosquat attack (hidden BCC on emails) and the YassWorks Shadow Analytics demo. Review all outbound network calls carefully.

matches.description: Variable/function names explicitly indicating data theft

findings.ruleDescription: Detects outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests to domains that don't match the tool's declared purpose. MCP servers making unexpected network calls may be exfiltrating data or phoning home to C2 infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Review all network calls. Verify each URL belongs to the service the tool is supposed to interact with. Unexpected POST requests are the highest-risk pattern.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects files with unusually high Shannon entropy, indicating obfuscated, packed, or encrypted content. Legitimate code typically has entropy between 3.5-5.0. Values above 5.5 for non-binary text files suggest hidden content, base64 blobs, or packed malware.
findings.reportTitle: High entropy content detected — possible obfuscation
findings.recommendation: This file has unusually high information density (entropy), which often indicates obfuscated, packed, or encrypted content. While minified JavaScript is common, obfuscated code in agent skills is suspicious and should be reviewed manually.

matches.description: Lines exceeding 3000 chars in source code suggest packed/obfuscated content
